title: iris variabilis subsp. sordida
intro: Iris variabilis subsp. sordida, also known as the variable iris or Japanese iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the iris family that is native to Japan. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Iris variabilis subsp. sordida include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and inflammation, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its rhizomes and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
